post_rendered:
Mafia Game 265
Job uPick Mafia

  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • Day chat will be enabled via an AoPS private forum.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • Do not imitate the mods.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>", or c or code text: 
      Vote: <insert player here>
      . Type usernames exactly, although capitalization doesn’t matter so long as the “Vote:” part is capitalized right. There is one exception, l and I are treated as the same.
    • Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang" or: 
      Vote: No Hang
      .
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though you have the ability to do so. Unvote in the format "Vote: Unvote" or: 
      Vote: Unvote
      . An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • If the deadline is met the player with the most votes is hanged. If there is a tie the player who received the votes first is hanged. If No Hang is part of the tie, No Hang occurs.
    • After the 48 hour mark in a day, If more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • After the 48 hour mark in a day, If at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • The moment the deadline passes, it will be twilight and no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once I (or the comod) post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • Each day will be 240 hours (10 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion.
    • The deadlines will be based on Central Timezone.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 240 hours has occurred.
    • The night deadline will be 48 hours after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA, you will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and they are not in V/LA, or a player has received too many PM prods, they will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • If a player is going to be V/LA, they must PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • Do not use inappropriate language or make personal attacks.
    • Be mature.
    • The game is to not be considered over until the mod or co-mod declares it as such.
    • Don't intentionally/purposely gamethrow. If you believe you can no longer win talk to me in your role pm.
    • For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, they may be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • I (NDMath) am the moderator of Mafia Game 265. WisteriaV is the comod of Mafia Game 265.
    • This brown (#974E03) is my mod color. Do not post in anything too similar. This green (#6c8) is WisteriaV's mod color. Do not post in anything too similar.
    • The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. The co-mod the second to last say.
    • Mafia fake claims will be treated symmetrically to town roles.

  • Read all of the rules
Story
It had been a month since the debacle at Suspectum Industries. Which although did not relate to the citizens in the town across the river, they had read about it in the newspaper and thought just that, it couldn't relate. Nobody would seek to harm their town or them, that was a problem in other towns but not this one. Of course, that mindset did not last when the only Town Firefighter, WisteriaV, vanished. It was clear she had put up a fight and was likely captured somewhere.

Multiple urgent problems were immediately apparent to the townspeople. Not only were a group of bad people present, they weren't just techies, and quite possibly they could be members of the mafia. And without the town's only firefighter, nobody could stop them from just lighting people's houses on fire in the night. Thirteen prominent townspeople quickly organized. They were determined to deal with the threat facing the town if it was the last thing they did.


Setup
As this game is a upick, when signing up for the game, each player will have submitted a list of 3 jobs to the co-mod, WisteriaV. Alignments will be randomized, and then a setup designed so that each player gets one of their submitted jobs as their role name.

This game also has a known special mechanic, income. Each player will earn a set number of coins each night as their income. During the day, extra votes for that dayphase only can be purchased for a number of coins equal to the day number. Such purchases will first be processed at the 48 hour mark of the day, and can at the latest be purchased at the 96 hour mark of the day. Extra votes adjust the threshhold for a majority, and purchases will remain anonymous until utilized. There will most likely be other, role specific, ways to interact with income.

The setup is closed, but the alignment ratio is 10 town and 3 mafia.

The action resolution for this game is Reasonable Action Resolution.
Private Chats are via AoPS forum with daychat enabled.
No action may self-target unless stated otherwise in the player's role pm.
Example Role PM wrote:
Welcome WisteriaV to [url=]Mafia Game 265[/url].

You are a Town Fire Fighter named Karis.

Growing up, Karis saw how devastating fires could be, whether it was hillsides turned black by the flames, or the smoke causing the air quality to be so bad people wouldn’t go outside. She had always wanted to have a job that would help people, and what better way than being a firefighter? She now has had extensive training in both fighting fires and emergency response making her well prepared for dangers to the town.

Fire Protection: Each night you may target a player to attempt to prevent any negative effects on that player.
Income: Each night you make 1 coin in income per 4 living players at the start of the night.

Confirm by responding with to this pm with your alignment. You win when all mafia are dead.

  • Tacostorm2 Scipio1 Tacostorm2 - Mafia Judge

post_rendered:
Flames danced all around the house. Their orange, red, and yellow tongues flickered and cast the entire street in a warm glow. People were fleeing from their houses, frantic and unsure what exactly they should do. A house was burning, but their firefighter was gone. Remi just stood there. Her eyes were glued to ever-growing flames from down the street, dazed. She was tired. It was what, 2 AM? 3? Whatever the time, she was not fully awake or thinking straight. She only snapped out of her trance when the sound of an ambulance came blaring down the street.

“You should leave!” A person said through a rolled down window. A sick feeling rose in Remi’s stomach when she realized the person was right.
But where... She didn’t even finish the thought and sprinted for her car. As she drove away, she could still see the flames in the rear-view mirror. Then she turned a corner and the flames were out of sight. A glow was still visible, though, and smoke rose from the area. I hope the guy who lives there is okay... She thought as she coughed. The smoke stung her throat every time she inhaled. No. I hope this is all a dream.

Some others had vowed to defend the town until the end. From what? To Remi it wasn't clear. But she had bigger problems to deal with, like finding a job. That was why she had come to this town in the first place, but it was too dangerous. Way too dangerously. Just leave all this chaos behind you.

Day 1 will start tomorrow evening

[color=#6c8]Flames danced all around the house. Their orange, red, and yellow tongues flickered and cast the entire street in a warm glow. People were fleeing from their houses, frantic and unsure what exactly they should do. A house was burning, but their firefighter was gone. Remi just stood there. Her eyes were glued to ever-growing flames from down the street, dazed. She was tired. It was what, 2 AM? 3? Whatever the time, she was not fully awake or thinking straight. She only snapped out of her trance when the sound of an ambulance came blaring down the street.
 
“You should leave!” A person said through a rolled down window. A sick feeling rose in Remi’s stomach when she realized the person was right.
[i]But where...[/i] She didn’t even finish the thought and sprinted for her car. As she drove away, she could still see the flames in the rear-view mirror. Then she turned a corner and the flames were out of sight. A glow was still visible, though, and smoke rose from the area. [i]I hope the guy who lives there is okay...[/i] She thought as she coughed. The smoke stung her throat every time she inhaled. [i]No. I hope this is all a dream.[/i]

Some others had vowed to defend the town until the end. [i]From what?[/i] To Remi it wasn't clear. But she had bigger problems to deal with, like finding a job. That was why she had come to this town in the first place, but it was too dangerous. Way too dangerously. [i]Just leave all this chaos behind you.[/i]

[b]Day 1 will start tomorrow evening[/b]

post_rendered:
some, probs most tbh, of the prs in this game are trash as expected anyways the claims are worth more than the ability and provide a springboard for discussion etc etc

aops has an obsession with not claiming until people can avoid it but severely underestimates the value of bucket-style strategies where prs get their utility out of their claim vs their ability, even if it means the prs kill them early

there’s a reason the champs meta in s8 champs (and, let’s be real, mu will always be a better mafia site than aops) was bucketing, and that was with actually sort of good prs like 1 shot vig, some sort of doctor, and a motion detector

the only difference here is that the setup is closed but some of the fundamental ideas behind bucketing still apply, with the only difference being that only the confirmable prs claim

some, probs most tbh, of the prs in this game are trash as expected anyways the claims are worth more than the ability and provide a springboard for discussion etc etc

aops has an obsession with not claiming until people can avoid it but severely underestimates the value of bucket-style strategies where prs get their utility out of their claim vs their ability, even if it means the prs kill them early

there’s a reason the champs meta in s8 champs (and, let’s be real, mu will always be a better mafia site than aops) was bucketing, and that was with actually sort of good prs like 1 shot vig, some sort of doctor, and a motion detector

the only difference here is that the setup is closed but some of the fundamental ideas behind bucketing still apply, with the only difference being that only the confirmable prs claim

These PRs are not really confirmable because either a town or a mafia can have a role that does X action. Their claims have no utility and you can't do bucketing because roles arent mutually exclusive which each other. At the end of the day all you end up with are the few useful PRs killed and the rest with some random abilities with no relevance that don't give any information about alignment.

// Functions for settings UI elements
let settings_ui = {
toggle: label => (name, value, settings_manager) => {
let checkbox_label = document.createElement('label');
let checkbox = document.createElement('input');
checkbox.type = 'checkbox';
checkbox.name = name;
checkbox.checked = value;
checkbox.addEventListener('change', e => settings_manager.set(name, e.target.checked));
checkbox_label.appendChild(checkbox);
checkbox_label.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' ' + label));
return checkbox_label;
},
select: (label, options) => (name, value, settings_manager) => {
let select_label = document.createElement('label');
select_label.innerText = label + ' ';
let select = document.createElement('select');
select.name = name;
for (let option of options) {
let option_element = document.createElement('option');
option_element.value = option[0];
option_element.innerText = option[1];
select.appendChild(option_element);
}
select.value = value;
select.addEventListener('change', e => settings_manager.set(name, e.target.value));
select_label.appendChild(select);
return select_label;
},
}

post_rendered:
Milo stared out the window into the night sky, or more accurately, the layer of smoke that hid the night sky. It was probably around midnight, but he couldn’t tell. As his eyes drifted is mind flitted back to what had happened that evening, and he squeezed his eyes shut as though it could block out the memory. It scared him seeing everyone so angry to the point that they would kill. It scared him even more knowing that there really was nowhere safe and no one you could truly trust. Let’s just hope we keep getting this right.

At some point, Milo fell asleep, even though he didn’t want to. He’d wanted to be ready in case anything happened, but it appeared nothing did when he awoke. No sirens. No shouts. It could almost pass for a normal day at 6:00 am, but then he looked at the news headline...

To sum up andy's posts, they are mostly vacuous and raises the question why would mafia want to kill someone who has added so little. The only other thing that stands out about andy's posts is that he is quite committed to believing Bob is mafia. If Bob was mafia, he'd kill andy to remove people hostile to him.

Now the question is, can Bob be mafia? Him leading the hang on joe gives Bob a lot of towncred. But it is still possible for Bob to be mafia.

The strategy that Bob as a mafia would employ is first he calls for roleclaims to fish for roles and figure out what the setup is like. Once he determines that there probably arent any investigative roles, he leads a hang on joe, making himself seem like a confirmed town. Then he proceeds to rely on town apathy to coast through the rest of the game and win. Silencing Chris would be part of this, removing an active voice and a chaotic influence who might derail things. I've seen this exact strategy play out before with a mafia member establishing themselves as the town leader by hanging their partner on day 1, then coasting through the rest of the game. The other people in the game said things along the lines of "If X is scum we've lost anyways" and made no efforts to stir themselves from their apathy.

This post from Bob pings a lot of those same vibes, which is why I didn't like this.

[quote=BobDBuilder321]does it need to be said that I just came off 3 extremely convincing wolf victories in the last month on mu, whose players are way better than aops and you guys would have no chance of winning if I was a wolf here anyways 
[/quote]

with 

[quote=BobDBuilder321]joe is probably a wolf[/quote]

Speaking of TMI, this post also reeks of it

[quote=lovematch13]Yay I'm back from Hawaii -- and I'm super tired being jet lagged 3 hours. imma read up on the stuff I missed. 
---------
Also I feel like joe is hiding something, somehow. If nothing happens soon, I'm gonna vote joe.[/quote]

The reasoning here is paper thin and looks to just be an excuse for hopping on joe.

I don't think we should hang Bob today, but the longer we go with him not getting killed the more suspect he is. His sudden desire to hang scannose also feels rather artificial with reasoning that feels more like something that he made up on the spot to justify voting for scannose, who is an easy target for Bob to ram through a quickhang on since no one really has strong feelings on scannose.

Today I'm leaning toward hanging lovematch though I'm still okay with hanging cai or galaxy, though my confidence in my galaxy read has gone down since yesterday.

The sun was setting, and the wisps of smoke that still lingered in the air made it spectacular. The sun itself was a vivid magenta and you could see the orangish-yellow beams of light cutting through the clouds, which were shades of light orange, pink, and purple. Watching it sink behind the mountains to the west, Milo could feel himself relaxing a little for the first time since the firefighter’s disappearance.

“Did you hear what happened on the other side of town?” Milo jumped at the voice, but it was just his next door neighbor, Aislin.

Crossing my fingers it’s good. But by her expression, he knew it wasn’t.

“Leo… They stormed the coffee shop just before it closed, thinking he was with the Mafia…”

Neither of them needed to finish, and Milo didn’t want to know more. There has to be a better way than this.

post_rendered:
itsjustWanderlust wrote:
Is there a standard night action order?
Like does mafia go first or smth

it's RAR
Unlike Natural Action Resolution, Reasonable Action Resolution does not try to place all night actions into one big list of "what happened" or "what didn't happen"; rather, it looks at particular effects that might happen (such as a particular player dying overNight or a particular investigation result), and works out whether or not it occurs via looking only at the actions that might be relevant to it. So it's possible for a roleblock to, e.g., succeed when determining whether player A dies, but fail when determining whether player B dies.

RAR also considers actions to be broken down into their components; for example, a Jailkeeper protects and roleblocks a target. In RAR, the protect and roleblock are considered separately, so (when determining whether some particular effect happens) one of them might succeed, and the other one might fail. Likewise, if one player roleblocks another, and the other player is taking two different actions, then the roleblock of each individual action is listed separately, treated as blocking action A and separately as blocking action B. (This splitting of roles into the simplest possible components is required for the algorithm to give a single, unambiguous result.)

To determine whether an effect occurs using RAR, you create a list of actions. First, list any actions that might (if they resolved) cause that effect. Then, add any actions that might (if they resolved) interfere with the actions you've already listed to the list. Keep going until you run out of actions to add, with the proviso that once an action has been placed on the list, it can't be placed there a second time (this guarantees that you will eventually stop listing actions). Once you finish making the list, resolve all the actions you've listed in the reverse order that you listed them; if that list of actions would produce the effect you're looking at, then it occurs, otherwise it doesn't


basically just like things happen unless something stops them from happening (full link in the OP)

post_rendered:
Milo was shaking. He didn’t know why. Maybe it was fear. Maybe it was because he hadn’t forced himself to eat. Maybe he was just tired. He pulled out his phone and started typing. Remi, I have no idea what to do. I’m scared. I’ve called the police, but they don’t seem to care about this little town. He pressed submit, then waited an agonizing 2 minutes for a response.

You could leave like I did.

If I could’ve, I would’ve done that by now. I feel like I’m losing it. I don’t understand anything anymore. Why are they doing this? Why are they handling this in this way? Why is no one stopping them?
And before you say I should do something to stop them, no. I don’t want to get myself killed.

I wasn’t going to. I think you should sleep if you can. It’s late, and I can practically feel you radiating tiredness through the text. We can talk more in the morning if you want, though.

Milo thought for a moment. Part of him still didn’t want to sleep, but he knew she was right. Alright, fine. Hopefully I can bring good news tomorrow.

When Milo awoke the next morning, he went to the town center and was barely surprised by what was being announced. Another house had been broken into last night, and the resident killed.

The town had been withering away before Milo's eyes for days. Many had fled, many had died, only a couple were left trying to prevent the mafia's complete takeover of the town. If they killed another innocent, surely it would be over. Milo was so scared of what would happen next if they were wrong.

But then Johnny spoke out to the small crowd.
"We have the necessary votes to end the day without anyone dying."
Everyone has shocked faces.
"You found me a cure!" shouts Miles.
"...Well besides you. The takeover of this little town is now inevitable."
lovematch13 has died.

lovematch13 was...
Role PM wrote:
Welcome lovematch13 to Mafia Game 265.

You are a Town Programmer named Miles.

Miles was Suspecum Industries’s replacement programmer, the previous one having been fired for helping the saboteurs hack into the payroll. As soon as he heard about the firefighter’s disappearance, he concluded that it must be the saboteurs or some of their buddies again, but hated how little he actually knew. To solve this, he started working on a small surveillance bot that would bring him some kind of information each night.

List.Visitors: Each odd-numbered night you may target a player to receive a list of all their visitors.
Get.Coins: Each even-numbered night, you may target a player to find out how many coins they have at the end of the night.
Target.self: You may target yourself with the above abilities and items you receive that you may otherwise not have been able to.
Suspectum Employee: You work at Suspectum Industries and may speak with other employees here{link} anytime while alive.
Income: You earn 4 coins per night.

Confirm by responding with to this pm with your alignment. You win when all mafia are dead.


Jurgen turns to Milo, "We need to leave. Now!"
"But Karis! We have got to save her."
"There's not a chance of that anymore, maybe you or Remi could do that another time but we can't stop the mafia anymore."
And with that Jurgen leaves and Elsie and Milo rushes after him. The mafia have taken over the town.
A proud William looks over the ruined town.

ChrisalonaLiverspur was Endgamed
NDMath wrote:
Welcome ChrisalonaLiverspur to Mafia Game 265.

You are a Town Soccer Analyst named Jurgen.

Jurgen is very passionate about Fargo, and although he isn't a professional player himself, he loves providing analysis and feedback to those that are. Of course, there are not any soccer fields in his town, so Jurgen has to travel for work frequently.

Travel: Each odd-numbered night you may choose to travel to a soccer match, temporarily exiting the game. You will return at the start of the next prime numbered day. On the night you leave any actions trying to target you will fail, and from then on nothing can target you until you return to the game.
Income: You earn 4 coins per night including when absent, unless you rejected your last opportunity to use travel.

Confirm by responding with to this pm with your alignment. You win when all mafia are dead.

MC42 was Endgamed
Role PM wrote:
Welcome MC42 to Mafia Game 265.

You are a Town Archeologist named Elsie.

Elsie was convinced there was something big under this very town. That is why she moved here a bit ago and stayed in spite of the mysterious loss of the firefighter. She is a very determined person and is not gonna allow for anything to get in her way, even the mafia.

Dig: Each night you may dig, the effect is unknown.
Income: You earn 3 coins per night.

Confirm by responding with to this pm with your alignment. You win when all mafia are dead.

itsjustWanderlust Survived
Role PM wrote:
Welcome itsjustWanderlust to Mafia Game 265.

You are a Mafia Non-Farm Animal Caretaker named Johnny.

From a young age, Johnny has always loved animals. But he's not a typical farm boy, looking to animals beyond cattle and chickens. All of his animals are highly trained, able to follow a command from Johnny but nobody else.

Animals: Each night you can order exactly one of your animals you have not yet used to target another player. To action investigatives, it will look like the animal performed the action instead of you.
Snake: Bites target player, poisoning them, and they will die at the end of the following day unless treated.
Black Bear: Blocks target player in their house, preventing them from taking any action including earning income.
Chimpanzee: Steals all of target player's coins besides income earned the same night.
Fly: Can not be noticed by other abilities, and informs you of target players role pm.
Hawk: Informs you of all players the target player used an action on that night.
Owl: Copies the ability of an animal, besides Snake, that you have already used.
Income: You earn 2 coins per night.

Confirm by responding with to this pm with your alignment. You win on the death of the ninth townie, or when nothing can prevent it.

Tacostorm2 Survived
Role PM wrote:
Welcome Tacostorm2 to Mafia Game 265.

You are a Mafia Judge named William.

William has studied law for many, many years, but that doesn't mean he follows it. He has judged several cases and so has a bit of insider information on the town. The last case he had was Suspectum Industries attorneys suing a journalism company for defamation.

Innocent Ruling: Each night you may target a player that you did not target with this ability the previous numbered night. That player will be announced as off limits the next day and unable to be voted for. At the end of the day your faction will learn that player's full role.
Informed: Information will be removed upon death: The only investigatives that receive results of full roles are possessed by the mafia faction. Suspectum Industries is in this game, in the form of a neighborhood that employees can talk to each other in.
Income: You earn 6 coins per night.

Confirm by responding with to this pm with your alignment. You win on the death of the ninth townie, or when nothing can prevent it.


Congrats to hellomynameisjoe, itsjustWanderlust, and Tacostorm2 for winning mafia game 265! Everyone can now post.

Scumchat: Scumchat
Suspectum Industries: Suspectum Industries

Setup Design
Of the job submissions, one was ruled out because I wasn't calling a role that, two for not being jobs, two for my inability to find any information on them. From there it was using what I could better come up with roles for, and avoiding repeats (three people submitted engineer, and two submitted judge).

-Clock Fixer: Of the submitted roles I immediately knew I wanted to include the Clock Fixer because I had though about a similar day manipulation mechanic in the past. Noon was purely a red herring ability, although also made the Clock Fixer pretty verifiable in terms of that was their role regardless of alignment. Role played out pretty much as intended.
-Soccer Analyst: Hadn't really felt this job made sense with the others, however I liked it more than the other submissions so had it play off of the Clock Fixer. Curious what the player perspective is on the absent mechanic, don't think it was interesting enough to justify someone not getting to play for a day.
-Happiness Advocate: Due to the other 'vocal' abilities in the setup (Clock Fixer, Professional Mourner, Judge, Salesperson etc.) decided to keep the happiness advocate's ability not noticable, and felt a rolestopper fit the theming.
-CEO: Initial plan was if someone submitted three president like roles to give them a weak pr and say they donated most of their income, but with the CEO submission played off the op flavor I had to use it. Originally there wasn't going to be anyone starting in the neighborhood, but Wisteria suggested the accountant should be there to which I also added the programmer so there wouldn't just be one player d1. fdas blocking the recruitment of the Salesperson night 1 definitely led to confusion for town on how this mechanic worked. I also forgot that I need a big paragraph at the top of each neighborhood, definitely won't forget in the future.
-Accountant: Counting coins seemed like a pretty natural ability. I wasn't expecting them and the programmer to immediately assume eachother town, but this belief did get corrected when the neighborhood was explained to the rest of the game.
-Programmer: Strongest town pr I think, although mafia told the truth about coins and both watcher shots missed.
-Professional Mourner: Weakest role in the setup, probably should've gotten more coins. Galaxy also kept presubmitting actions which wasn't intended play and was definitely hindering the coins they could get out of the role.
-Salesperson: Decided to overlap their items for sale with other items in the game, to create interactions where some communication needed to occur for them to properly be utilized.
-Archeologist: Went with them not knowing what they'd find to fit theming and was curious how such a role would play out. What was found was fully pre-determined.
-Barista: Ended up using mathjay's submissions for ninjawhiz, and this trio of jobs was clearly mathjay's (also could tell Chris's submissions but otherwise didn't) and didn't really want to give e-boy to someone that hadn't submitted it so went with barista. I think I overestimated the usefulness of barista, since forgot it couldn't actually block the factional kill ever unless there was only one mafia left.

Overall, I do think the setup ended up being a bit scumsided. In part because I worried too much about town being able to speculate which roles belonged to which alignment.

-Non-Farm Animal Caretake: I definitely misinterpreted what this was intended to mean lol. A JoAT made alot of sense to me for this role, and this role held the majority of the scumteam's power. I had thought it was very likely a kill would be blocked at some point so felt fine with the extra killpower from snake, however this was probably a mistake.
-Judge: Spent quite a while debating over whether this should be a 5 or 6 coin role, don't think that actually mattered.
-Florist: This role was designed prior to rolling alignments (besides the income amount, which probably would've been a two had it been town) and I left the ability as is to give a mafia role that couldn't be speculated as mafia as all (and functioned pretty much like a mafia goon). However, the role got claimed weirdly and was the one mafia role speculated as mafia.

The mafia wincon was written how it was to prevent the scenario of the last townie outvoting the last mafia just off of coins (primarily for the case of florist, but the other two roles as well), but still allowing a wealthy town comeback from even numbers if a kill got blocked.


This was an enjoyable game to mod. Town was pretty close to winning on day #2 and especially #3 since a scumhang on either of those days leaves the remaining mafia member in a lot of trouble. Particularly enjoyed town piecing together the mechanics of dead player's roles.

-Archeologist: Went with them not knowing what they'd find to fit theming and was curious how such a role would play out. What was found was fully pre-determined.
-Barista: Ended up using mathjay's submissions for ninjawhiz, and this trio of jobs was clearly mathjay's (also could tell Chris's submissions but otherwise didn't) and didn't really want to give e-boy to someone that hadn't submitted it so went with barista. I think I overestimated the usefulness of barista, since forgot it couldn't actually block the factional kill ever unless there was only one mafia left.

Overall, I do think the setup ended up being a bit scumsided. In part because I worried too much about town being able to speculate which roles belonged to which alignment.

-Non-Farm Animal Caretake: I definitely misinterpreted what this was intended to mean lol. A JoAT made alot of sense to me for this role, and this role held the majority of the scumteam's power. I had thought it was very likely a kill would be blocked at some point so felt fine with the extra killpower from snake, however this was probably a mistake.
-Judge: Spent quite a while debating over whether this should be a 5 or 6 coin role, don't think that actually mattered.
-Florist: This role was designed prior to rolling alignments (besides the income amount, which probably would've been a two had it been town) and I left the ability as is to give a mafia role that couldn't be speculated as mafia as all (and functioned pretty much like a mafia goon). However, the role got claimed weirdly and was the one mafia role speculated as mafia.

The mafia wincon was written how it was to prevent the scenario of the last townie outvoting the last mafia just off of coins (primarily for the case of florist, but the other two roles as well), but still allowing a wealthy town comeback from even numbers if a kill got blocked.[/hide]

This was an enjoyable game to mod. Town was pretty close to winning on day #2 and especially #3 since a scumhang on either of those days leaves the remaining mafia member in a lot of trouble. Particularly enjoyed town piecing together the mechanics of dead player's roles.

post_rendered:
I actually wanted to die

I was planning on doing it so that I made it exceptionally clear who I was targeting. If I am right, the scum has no choice but to kill me, signaling to everyone else who the scum is. If I don't die, I can conclude the person I was "targeting" was safe. In either case I don't use the read, and on the next night I actually read someone else. This only works if there's only one scum, because with two they can afford to not kill me and defend against my read.
